name: upgrade_names_oedi
description: get_upgrade_names — was broken before 2026-04-26 fix on two counts: (1) f-string interpolation of self.up_table (a Subquery) produced malformed `FROM SELECT * FROM ...` SQL with no enclosing parens; (2) the method assumed an `apply_upgrade.upgrade_name` column that doesn't exist on OEDI schemas. Fixed to use SA construction and gracefully degrade to `CAST(NULL AS VARCHAR) AS upgrade_name` when the name column is absent — so the dict shape stays consistent across schemas.

name: upgrades_csv_three_buildings_upgrade1
description: get_upgrades_csv for upgrade=1 restricted to a tiny known building_id list. Mirrors results_csv_three_buildings on the upgrade-table side.

name: buildings_by_locations_state
description: get_buildings_by_locations: list bs-keys whose location_col=$STATE_COL_BASELINE is in ['CO']. Different code path from restrict — uses bs_key_cols projection and IN-list filter directly.

name: results_csv_three_buildings
description: get_results_csv restricted to a tiny known building_id list. Pins the SELECT-* shape of the baseline metadata projection used by basic_usage.ipynb. Bounded to 3 buildings so the data parquet stays small (results_csv has ~150 columns, full state would be 100+ MB).

name: distinct_vals_state_baseline
description: get_distinct_vals on the baseline-table state column. Pins the simple SELECT DISTINCT shape — used by notebooks to enumerate categorical values before building restricts. The column name differs by schema (resstock=`in.state`, comstock=`state`); resolved via $STATE_COL_BASELINE.

name: distinct_count_state_baseline
description: get_distinct_count on the baseline-table state column. Pins the COUNT-with-weighted-sum shape (sample_count + weighted_count per category).
